contentSnippet: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

title: Fizz takes three in Mumbai rout
Sindhupalchok steps up forest fire prevention with fire lines in over 30 community forests
contentSnippet: District forest office accelerates preparedness as rising heat and wind increase wildfire risks; 82 hectares already damaged.

title: Birgunj begins demolition drive on encroached structures
contentSnippet: Around 1,200 private buildings and 29 government structures fall within a 25-metre road widening plan from Gandak Chowk to Miteri Bridge.

title: Celebrating women-led enterprises at SHEpreneur Awards 2026
contentSnippet: Hatti Hatti Nepal honours women-led ventures making a difference at the local level.

title: Humanoid robots race past humans in Beijing half-marathon, showing rapid advances
contentSnippet: Most entrants self-navigate 21-km course as winning robot finishes in 50 minutes, beating human record time.

title: BP Highway blocked after rain washes away diversions in Kavre
contentSnippet: Traffic halted since 11pm Saturday after multiple diversion points along the Katunje–Mangaltar stretch were swept away, with vehicles stranded at several locations.

title: Kathmandu police map 1,097 repeat offenders
contentSnippet: Profile list covers people linked to gangs, theft and fraud, aiming to guide future investigations.

title: Dhami, Kakshapati win at Immortal Fighting Championship in India
contentSnippet: Two Nepali fighters, Sangita Dhami and Kunjan Kakshapati, defeated Indian fighters Aarya Choudhary and Inzamam Ul Huck, respectively, in the inaugural edition of the tournament held in Bengaluru on Saturday night.

title: Nepal business delegation pitches ‘hydro-to-data’ vision in Washington meetings
contentSnippet: The US Trade and Development Agency expressed interest in potentially supporting feasibility studies for such projects, according to the delegation.

title: Russian authorities detain suspect over St. Petersburg cafe blast

contentSnippet: Bulgarian voters are going to the polls for the eighth time in five years amid a long-running political crisis. A new left-leaning, pro-Russian party under former President Radev is seen as favorite to win.
